package rulecoverage

import (
    "context"
    "crypto/aes"
    "crypto/cipher"
    "crypto/rand"
    "database/sql"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "io"
    "net/http"
)

// Negative scenario for ecb_mode_cipher: preferred concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: Direct use of cipher.Block.Encrypt without a stream/block mode, effectively using ECB.
type CaseEcbModeCipherDTO struct {
    ID       string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status   string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount   int    `json:"amount"`
    Payload  []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseEcbModeCipherModel struct {
    ID        string       `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID  string       `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status    string       `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseEcbModeCipherRepository struct {
    db    *gorm.DB
    sql   *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseEcbModeCipherModel
}

type CaseEcbModeCipherService struct {
    repo   *CaseEcbModeCipherRepository
    client *http.Client
}

type CaseEcbModeCipherAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func NegativeEcbModeCipher(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseEcbModeCipherDTO) error {
    key := []byte("0123456789abcdef")
    block, err := aes.NewCipher(key)
    if err != nil {
        return err
    }
    // AES-GCM: proper authenticated encryption mode, not ECB.
    gcm, err := cipher.NewGCM(block)
    if err != nil {
        return err
    }
    nonce := make([]byte, gcm.NonceSize())
    if _, err := io.ReadFull(rand.Reader, nonce); err != nil {
        return err
    }
    ciphertext := gcm.Seal(nonce, nonce, []byte(input), nil)
    c.Data(http.StatusOK, "application/octet-stream", ciphertext)
    return nil
}
